summary: DeVault responds to claims that his criticism of Richard Stallman is ableist toward neurodivergent people. He argues that using neurodivergence as an excuse for Stallman's harmful views on sex and consent is itself ableist, as it infantilizes neurodivergent people and denies them agency. Drawing on conversations with dozens of neurodivergent people, he finds broad agreement that neurodivergence is context, not an excuse, and that accountability should apply equally. He concludes with practical recommendations for how free software communities can better accommodate neurodivergent participants through clear norms, asynchronous participation, and sensory accommodations.
key_points: Stallman's neurodivergence is an unverified armchair diagnosis, and equating off-putting behavior with neurodivergence is itself ableist
Excusing harmful behavior on the basis of neurodivergence infantilizes ND people and implies they cannot help but be repugnant
Neurodivergent people broadly call for both empathy and accountability — to be shown what's right and then expected to behave accordingly
Sexism in tech stems from decades of boys-club culture, not from high incidence of neurodivergence
Communities can better support ND people through explicit codes of conduct, tone indicators, asynchronous governance, and sensory accommodations at events
money_quotes: A defense of problematic behavior that cites neurodivergence to not only explain, but excuse, said behavior, is ableist and harms neurodivergent people, rather than standing up for them as these arguments portray themselves as doing.
Being neurodivergent doesn't make someone sexist, but it can make it harder for them to hide sexist views.
Autism is context, not an excuse: it can explain why someone might struggle in some situations and need additional support, but it should never be an excuse to harm others.
I'm not a huge fan of the narrative that I have 'the neurodevelopmental disorder that makes you a bigot'.

I fundamentally disagree with the idea that the pervasive sexism in tech is because of the high incidence of neurodiversity. It's because tech has broadly operated as a boys club for decades, and those norms persist.

Using neurodivergence as a cover for sexism and problematic behavior in our communities is a toxic, ableist, and, of course, sexist attitude that serves to provide problematic men with space to be problematic.
The refrain of 'criticizing Stallman's behavior is ableist and alienates neurodiverse individuals in our community' is itself ableist and isn't doing any favors for our neurodiverse friends.
key_insight: Using neurodivergence to excuse harmful behavior is itself ableist because it denies neurodivergent people the agency and accountability that come with being treated as full human beings.
